content: Vector-borne diseases transmitted by arthropods cause significant global morbidity. Lyme disease (Borrelia burgdorferi): tick-borne (Ixodes), endemic to Northeast/Midwest US, Europe; stages: early localized (erythema migrans 'bull's-eye rash'), early disseminated (multiple EM lesions, facial palsy, meningitis, carditis), late (arthritis, encephalopathy). Malaria (Plasmodium species): mosquito-borne (Anopheles); P. falciparum most severe (cerebral malaria, organ failure); P. vivax/ovale have hypnozoites causing relapses. Dengue (flavivirus): Aedes mosquito; spectrum from mild fever to severe dengue (plasma leakage, hemorrhage, organ impairment); secondary infection with different serotype increases severe disease risk via antibody-dependent enhancement. Other: Zika (microcephaly in pregnancy), Chikungunya (severe arthralgia), West Nile (neuroinvasive disease).
